content.text: Perfect. Now let me create a comprehensive summary document. Let me compile all the information I've gathered: --- ## ROCKYBOT CODEBASE EXPLORATION SUMMARY ### 1. TOP-LEVEL STRUCTURE ``` /home/ubuntu/src/rockybot/ ├── README.md Project overview & quick-start guide ├── CLAUDE.md Internal project notes for Claude ├── CHANGELOG.md Version history (semver, conventional commits) ├── LICENSE MIT license ├── .env.example Configuration template with all env vars ├── package.json Root package (canonical version = 1.0.1) ├── docker-compose.yml Production 4-service stack ├── docker-compose.dev.yml Dev stack (single bot container, temp vault) ├── .github/ GitHub Actions workflows ├── docs/ User documentation │ ├── INSTALLATION.md Setup walkthrough for local & remote │ ├── WORKFLOW.md How to write research requests & use callouts │ ├── CONFIGURATION.md All env vars reference │ ├── NOTIFICATIONS.md Discord & Twilio WhatsApp setup │ ├── OBSIDIAN_SETUP.md Obsidian Sync & template plugin config │ └── DEVELOPMENT.md Testing & adding new watchers ├── services/ Core application code │ ├── bot/ Main polling bot & all 5 watchers │ ├── obsidian-bridge/ Vault file watcher → Quartz builder │ └── obsidian-headless/ Obsidian Sync daemon (remote only) ├── vault-scaffold/ Default vault structure & prompt templates ├── templates/ Obsidian template files ├── tests/ Unit & integration test suites ├── scripts/ Automation (release.sh for semver bumping) └── vault-scaffold/ └── research/ ├── inbox/ User research requests go here ├── processed/ Completed requests (archive) ├── *.md Prompts (research-, amend-, expand-, revise-, lint-) ├── lint-trigger.md Audit trigger file ├── journal.md (seeded but empty initially) └── password-managers/ Example completed research topic ``` --- ### 2. DATA MODEL **Note Storage Format:** - Markdown files with YAML frontmatter - File-based, no database — stored in Obsidian vault directory - All data flows through the vault; no separate state store **Standard Frontmatter Fields:** ```yaml --- tags: [research-request] # Tag to identify what kind of file created: 2026-04-17 # ISO date when created status: pending|processing|completed|error|awaiting-input updated: 2026-04-17 # Last modified date sources: # Bibliography (list of URLs + titles) - url: https://... title: "Source Title" related: ["[[wikilink]]", "[[other]]"] # Cross-references (optional) publish: true|false # Quartz publication flag --- ``` **Research Topic Structure:** ``` research/ ├── index.md Topic overview, TL;DR, sub-page index ├── options-comparison.md Sub-pages for aspects worth their own section ├── tradeoffs.md (user decides granularity) ├── self-hosting-guide.md └── ...other sub-pages ``` **Research Request (in inbox/):** - Markdown file with `status: pending` and `research-request` tag - Content is the seed note (topic, scope, context) - Watcher moves it to `processed/` when starting, updates status to `processing` - Final status set to `completed` or `awaiting-input` (if Claude needs clarification) **Callout Syntax (inline tasks):** ```markdown > [!claude] > Edit instruction text here > [!expand] > Brief for new sub-page to create > [!revise] > Context for corpus-wide revisions ``` **Note-to-Note Links:** - Obsidian wikilink format: `[[research/topic-name/page|display text]]` - No special ID system — just file paths relative to vault root - Backlinks inferred from mention in markdown files **Karpathy Ingest Pass:** - Final step of research inbox watcher - Claude re-reads existing research tree and adds backlinks from new topic to related existing pages - Described in `research/research-prompt.md` (Step 5) - Ensures new knowledge propagates backward into the knowledge graph, not just forward --- ### 3. SERVICES/COMPONENTS **Service Architecture: 4 Docker containers** | Service | Container | Role | Runs on | Port | |---|---|---|---|---| | `rockybot` (bot) | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot:latest` | Main watcher loop, runs all Claude tasks | Always | Internal | | `rockybot-bridge` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge:latest` | Watches vault, builds Quartz static site | Always | Internal | | `rockybot-headless` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless:latest` | Obsidian Sync daemon (remote deployments only) | Remote only | Internal | | `notes-web` | `nginx:alpine` | Serves built Quartz site | Always | `8080` | **Bot Watchers (all serialized by global mutex in `index.js`):** Each watcher runs every 10 minutes (default `INBOX_POLL_SECONDS=600`). Only one Claude invocation at a time. | Watcher | File | Trigger | Claude Task | Budget | Model | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | **inbox** | `inbox-watcher.js` | `research/inbox/*.md` with `status: pending` | Full multi-page research + Karpathy backlink pass | `$2.00` | `sonnet` | | **amend** | `amend-watcher.js` | `> [!claude]` callout in any research page | Targeted inline edit per instruction | `$0.50` | `haiku` | | **expand** | `expand-watcher.js` | `> [!expand]` callout | Create new sub-page, replace callout with wikilink | `$1.00` | `sonnet` | | **revise** | `revise-watcher.js` | `> [!revise]` callout in topic `index.md` | Corpus-level rewrite: find all wikilinked sub-pages, revise each in-place | `$4.00` | `sonnet` | | **lint** | `lint-watcher.js` | `status: pending` in `research/lint-trigger.md` | Full wiki audit: orphaned pages, broken wikilinks, write report | `$5.00` | `sonnet` | **Polling Loop:** - Main loop in `services/bot/src/index.js` - Global mutex (`globalRunning` flag) — only one Claude invocation at a time - Serial execution: `scanInbox()` → `scanAmendments()` → `scanExpands()` → `scanRevisions()` → `scanLint()` - Runs on interval (default 10 min), continues forever **Obsidian Bridge:** - Watches vault with chokidar (5s polling, 3s write-finish debounce) - Listens for `.md` file changes - Debounced call to Quartz builder - On each build: reads `publish: true` flags from topic `index.md` files, syncs published topics to Quartz, builds static site **Quartz Integration:** - Quartz v4.4.0 baked into bridge image at build time (pinned, no internet at runtime) - Bridge invokes `npx quartz build --output /quartz-output` - Serves generated static site via nginx on port `8080` - Wikilinks converted to HTML by Quartz; `.md` files become `.html` --- ### 4. FRONTEND: STATIC NOTES WEBSITE **Framework:** - Quartz (open-source static site generator for Obsidian vaults) - No dynamic frontend; all content is pre-rendered HTML, CSS, JavaScript **Generated Site Structure:** - Root index: auto-generated by bridge from published topics - Topic pages: `/notes.{domain}/{topic-slug}/` (published topics only) - Sub-pages: `/notes.{domain}/{topic-slug}/{page-slug}/` - Wikilinks: converted to relative HTML links - Full-text search, backlink graph, sidebar navigation (Quartz defaults) **How Notes & Links Render:** - Obsidian wikilinks `[[research/topic/page|text]]` → Quartz converts to `text` - Publish gate: only topics with `publish: true` in their `index.md` are included in Quartz build - Markdown processed to HTML; frontmatter stripped except tags - Quartz plugins: graph view, sidebar, breadcrumbs, search (configurable) **Webserver:** - nginx:alpine container - Simple config in `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` - Listens on `0.0.0.0:8080` - Serves `/usr/share/nginx/html/` (mapped to `/quartz-output/` from bridge) - Caching headers on static assets (7-day cache for JS, CSS, images) --- ### 5. API LAYER / CLAUDE INTEGRATION **Not a REST API** — rockybot uses the Claude CLI (`claude --print` subprocess), not a web API. **Claude Runner (`services/bot/src/claude-runner.js`):** - Spawns `claude --print ` subprocess (Claude Code CLI, installed at build time) - Collects stdout/stderr, resolves with `{ ok, stderr }` - Never rejects — returns error status so callers can inspect error type **Billing Strategy:** - Default: `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (pay-per-use) - Optional: `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` tries Pro/Max subscription first, falls back to API key on auth failure - Subscription auth failure handling: - If `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`: spawns `claude login` headlessly, posts OAuth URL to Discord as clickable button, waits for user decision (or 5-min timeout) before proceeding - Else: fires a throttled Discord alert (max 2x/day) and falls back to API key silently **Tools Passed to Claude:** - Inbox: `Bash,Edit,Read,Write,Glob,Grep,WebSearch,WebFetch` - Amend: `Edit,Read,Write,Glob,Grep,WebSearch,WebFetch` - Expand: `Edit,Read,Write,Glob,Grep,WebSearch,WebFetch` - Revise: `Edit,Read,Write,Glob,Grep,WebSearch,WebFetch` - Lint: `Edit,Read,Write,Glob,Grep` (no web access needed) **Prompts:** - Loaded from vault files: `research/*-prompt.md` - User-editable in Obsidian - Template tokens: `{{VAULT_PATH}}`, `{{SEED_CONTENTS}}`, `{{FILE_PATH}}`, `{{TRIGGER_FILE}}`, `{{SEED_PATH}}` - Built-in fallbacks if vault file missing (hardcoded in each watcher) **Budget Enforcement:** - `--max-budget-usd` flag passed to `claude` for API-key mode only - Subscription mode does not enforce budget (limitation of Claude CLI) - Separate budget per watcher: `RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD`, `AMEND_BUDGET_USD`, etc. - Default: `$2.00` (research), `$0.50` (amend), `$1.00` (expand), `$4.00` (revise), `$5.00` (lint) --- ### 6. DATABASE **No database.** - File-based only: vault is the entire state store - Markdown files + directory structure - All state persisted via file writes (`fs-extra` library) - No locks, no transactions, no ACID — relies on OS file atomicity and Obsidian Sync (or direct mount) for consistency **LiveSync (self-hosted sync alternative) not supported:** - CLAUDE.md explains why: Live Sync's conflict resolution can corrupt status fields during concurrent watchers - Obsidian Sync (official paid service) is the recommended remote sync solution - Local setup: direct directory mount (e.g., NFS, SSHFS, or same-machine mount) --- ### 7. DOCKER/DEPLOYMENT **Production Stack (`docker-compose.yml`):** ```yaml obsidian-headless: # Obsidian Sync daemon (remote only) rockybot-bridge: # Vault watcher + Quartz builder notes-web: # nginx serving Quartz output rockybot: # Main polling bot ``` **Docker Images:** - Canonical source: https://ghcr.io/mrwong/ (GitHub Container Registry) - Built from GitHub Actions on tag push - Tags: `latest` (stable), `dev`/`edge` (dev branch), `vX.Y.Z` (releases), `vX.Y` (latest patch) **Volumes:** - `./data/vault:/vault` — shared Obsidian vault - `./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output` — generated static site - `./data/obsidian-config:/config` — headless Obsidian config (remote only) - `~/.claude:/home/ubuntu/.claude` — Claude subscription session (optional, Pro/Max billing) **Environment Variables:** - All configuration via `.env` (copied from `.env.example`) - Sourced into `docker compose` via `--env-file` - No CLI flags; everything is env vars **Dev Stack (`docker-compose.dev.yml`):** - Single `rockybot-dev` container - Temp vault at `/tmp/rockybot-dev-vault` (isolated, never touches live vault) - Live mounts `services/bot/src` for hot-reload on JS changes - Tight budget caps for safety: `$0.10` per watcher, except lint `$0.50` - Uses haiku model for all watchers (cheaper) - Poll interval: 60s (faster iteration) - `DRY_RUN=true` option for testing without calling Claude **Headless Obsidian Service:** - Image: `obsidian-headless` - Runs `obsidian-headless` (npm package) - First-time setup requires interactive auth; stores token in `/config` - Syncs vault with Obsidian Sync cloud continuously - Runs ahead of bridge in `depends_on` chain **Release Process:** - `./scripts/release.sh` — interactive semver bump + CHANGELOG + git tag - Reads recent commits, proposes bump based on conventional commit prefixes - User confirms, script updates `package.json` and `CHANGELOG.md`, creates tag - Tag push triggers GitHub Actions → builds & pushes Docker images to GHCR - Images tagged with version number (e.g., `1.2.3`) and `latest` --- ### 8. KEY FILE PATHS BY SUBSYSTEM **Bot Core:** - `/services/bot/src/index.js` — main loop, global mutex, watcher orchestration - `/services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — Claude subprocess spawning, auth fallback, error classification - `/services/bot/src/notifier.js` — Discord & Twilio notifications (status updates, auth alerts) - `/services/bot/src/seeder.js` — copies vault scaffold on startup - `/services/bot/src/logger.js` — structured logging - `/services/bot/package.json` — canonical version source (1.0.1) **Watchers:** - `/services/bot/src/inbox-watcher.js` — research request processing, file staging - `/services/bot/src/amend-watcher.js` — inline `[!claude]` tasks - `/services/bot/src/expand-watcher.js` — `[!expand]` callout handling - `/services/bot/src/revise-watcher.js` — corpus-level revision - `/services/bot/src/lint-watcher.js` — wiki audit & repair **Interactive Auth (optional):** - `/services/bot/src/discord-bot.js` — Discord WebSocket bot, button handling - `/services/bot/src/login-runner.js` — spawns `claude login`, captures OAuth URL **Obsidian Bridge:** - `/services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` — main loop, chokidar watcher setup - `/services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` — Quartz build orchestration, publish gate - `/services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` — static site serving config - `/services/obsidian-bridge/package.json` — bridge version (kept in sync with bot) **Headless Obsidian:** - `/services/obsidian-headless/Dockerfile` - `/services/obsidian-headless/entrypoint.sh` - `/services/obsidian-headless/setup.sh` **Vault Scaffold (baked into bot image):** - `/vault-scaffold/research/research-prompt.md` — prompt template for inbox research - `/vault-scaffold/research/amend-prompt.md` — prompt template for `[!claude]` tasks - `/vault-scaffold/research/expand-prompt.md` — prompt template for `[!expand]` - `/vault-scaffold/research/revise-prompt.md` — prompt template for `[!revise]` - `/vault-scaffold/research/lint-prompt.md` — prompt template for lint pass - `/vault-scaffold/research/index.md` — research index (seeded, user-editable) - `/vault-scaffold/research/lint-trigger.md` — audit trigger file - `/vault-scaffold/research/inbox/example-research-request.md` — template example - `/vault-scaffold/research/processed/` — archive for completed requests - `/vault-scaffold/research/password-managers/` — example completed research **Tests:** - `/tests/unit/inbox-watcher.test.js` — unit tests (no Claude) - `/tests/unit/claude-runner.test.js` - `/tests/unit/seeder.test.js` - `/tests/integration/` — (uses real Claude, costs money) **Configuration & Docs:** - `/.env.example` — full reference, all vars with defaults - `/docs/CONFIGURATION.md` — detailed env var descriptions - `/docs/INSTALLATION.md` — setup walkthrough - `/docs/WORKFLOW.md` — how to use (callout syntax, status machine, troubleshooting) - `/docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` — adding new watchers, testing, release process - `/CLAUDE.md` — internal project notes for Claude **Dockerfiles:** - `/services/bot/Dockerfile` — bot image build - `/services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile` — bridge image (includes Quartz) - `/services/obsidian-headless/Dockerfile` — headless Obsidian --- ### 9. CRITICAL ARCHITECTURAL PATTERNS **Global Mutex Pattern:** - All watchers compete for a single global lock in `index.js` - No concurrent Claude runs; budget is the bottleneck - Serial execution: prevents vault conflicts, ensures consistent ordering - Each watcher polls the vault independently within the lock **Prompt Template Pattern:** - Every watcher loads its prompt from the vault (`research/*-prompt.md`) - User can edit in Obsidian to change behavior - Template tokens get substituted before passing to Claude - Built-in fallback if file missing (ensures bot works even on first boot) **File-Based State Pattern:** - Status stored in frontmatter (`status: pending|processing|completed|error`) - File moves between directories as state changes (`inbox/` → `processed/`) - Status update + move must be atomic to survive crashes - Inbox watcher handles crash recovery: if a file is stuck in `processing`, it resets to `pending` **Subscription Fallback Pattern:** - Try Pro/Max first (if configured) - On auth failure, optional interactive Discord flow (if enabled) - On rate limit, silent fallback to API key - On other errors, throw and let caller decide - Separate bill modes allow users to layer budget: subscription for expensive tasks, API key for rate-limited fallback **Callout Detection Pattern:** - Regex: `^>\s*\[!\]` (block-level, not inline in prose) - Each watcher finds its callout type, processes files, removes callouts after completion - No nested callouts or multi-callout files (simple, robust design) **Vault Sync Pattern:** - Files written by Claude are picked up by Obsidian Sync (or direct mount) - Changes sync to user devices in near-real-time - Obsidian bridge watches for changes and rebuilds Quartz site - No server-side state; all files are user-accessible in Obsidian --- ### 10. CONFIGURATION ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES **API & Billing:** - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — Required unless using subscription - `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE` — Try Pro/Max first before API key - `CLAUDE_BIN` — Path to `claude` executable (default: `claude`) **Paths:** - `VAULT_PATH` — Mounted vault directory (default: `/vault`) - `QUARTZ_OUTPUT` — Built site output dir (default: `/quartz-output`) - `DOMAIN_NAME` — Domain for site URL (default: `localhost`) **Polling:** - `INBOX_POLL_SECONDS` — How often to scan vault (default: `600` = 10 min) **Spending Limits:** - `RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD` — Inbox budget (default: `$2.00`) - `AMEND_BUDGET_USD` — Amend budget (default: `$0.50`) - `EXPAND_BUDGET_USD` — Expand budget (default: `$1.00`) - `REVISE_BUDGET_USD` — Revise budget (default: `$4.00`) - `LINT_BUDGET_USD` — Lint budget (default: `$5.00`) **Model Selection:** - `AMEND_MODEL` — Amend model (default: `haiku`) - `EXPAND_MODEL` — Expand model (default: `sonnet`) - `REVISE_MODEL` — Revise model (default: `sonnet`) - `LINT_MODEL` — Lint model (default: `sonnet`) - (Inbox always uses `sonnet`) **Notifications:** - `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL` — One-way status updates (optional) - `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH` — Enable interactive OAuth in Discord (optional) - `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN` — Discord bot token (required if interactive auth enabled) - `DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID` — Channel ID for auth prompts (required if interactive auth enabled) - `DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES` — Timeout before auto-fallback (default: `5`) - `TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID`, `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`, `TWILIO_FROM`, `TWILIO_TO` — WhatsApp notifications (optional) **Development:** - `DRY_RUN` — Detect but don't call Claude (default: `false`) - `DEBUG` — Enable debug logging (optional) --- ### 11. TESTING **Unit Tests (no Claude required):** - `tests/unit/inbox-watcher.test.js` — status parsing, frontmatter extraction, file detection - `tests/unit/claude-runner.test.js` — error classification, fallback logic - `tests/unit/seeder.test.js` — vault scaffold seeding - Run with `npm test` **Integration Tests (uses real Claude):** - `tests/integration/` — full end-to-end flows - Run with `npm run test:integration` (costs money) - Disabled by default --- ### 12. VERSIONING & RELEASE **Semantic Versioning:** - Current version: `1.0.1` (stored in `services/bot/package.json`) - Major: breaking changes (removed env vars, incompatible format) - Minor: new features (new watchers, new callout types, new env vars) - Patch: bug fixes, refactors, tests, CI changes **Conventional Commits:** - `feat:` → minor bump (or `feat!:` for major) - `fix:` → patch bump (or `fix!:` for major) - `refactor:`, `perf:` → patch - `test:`, `chore:`, `docs:` → no version bump **Release Process:** 1. Run `./scripts/release.sh` on `main` branch 2. Script shows commits since last tag, proposes bump 3. User confirms or overrides 4. Script updates `package.json`, `CHANGELOG.md`, creates tag 5. Push tag to origin → GitHub Actions builds & pushes Docker images 6. Tag format: `vX.Y.Z` 7. Image tags: `latest`, `main`, `X.Y.Z`, `X.Y` (pinned) **Branch Strategy:** - `dev` → `edge`/`dev` images (pre-release) - `main` → `latest` image (stable) - Feature branches → PR to `dev` → PR to `main` when stable - Never push directly to `main` --- ### 13. KEY DESIGN DECISIONS & RATIONALE 1. **No Database:** Vault files are the source of truth. User has direct access in Obsidian. No synchronization needed. 2. **Global Mutex:** Serializes Claude invocations. Prevents vault conflicts; API budget is bottleneck, not parallelism. 3. **File-Based State:** Status lives in frontmatter. Survives container restarts, crashes, and cluster failures. 4. **Prompt Templates in Vault:** Users can customize behavior without redeploying. Prompts are readable, editable, versionable. 5. **Subscription + API Key Fallback:** Allows layering: expensive tasks on subscription, fallback to pay-per-use under load. 6. **Obsidian Sync (not LiveSync):** First-party sync service guarantees compatibility. LiveSync's conflict resolution breaks status fields. 7. **Quartz for Static Site:** Zero-cost hosting (GitHub Pages, S3, CDN). Content stays in user's vault; publishing is opt-in (`publish: true` flag). 8. **Discord Notifications:** Async, non-blocking. Webhook for status; bot for interactive auth. Optional; no external dependency unless configured. 9. **Karpathy Ingest Pass:** Automated backlink creation ensures knowledge graph stays dense and interconnected. Final step of research requests. 10. **Callout Syntax:** Simple regex detection (`^>\s*\[!type\]`). No special parsers, no markdown AST. Robust, user-friendly. --- This summary should give you enough detail to design new features, add watchers, or extend functionality without re-exploring the codebase. Key files to start with: `services/bot/src/index.js` (orchestration), `claude-runner.js` (Claude integration), `inbox-watcher.js` (example watcher pattern), and `vault-scaffold/research/research-prompt.md` (prompt format).

content.text: Perfect. Now let me create a comprehensive summary document. Let me compile all the information I've gathered: --- ## ROCKYBOT CODEBASE EXPLORATION SUMMARY ### 1. TOP-LEVEL STRUCTURE ``` /home/ubuntu/src/rockybot/ ├── README.md Project overview & quick-start guide ├── CLAUDE.md Internal project notes for Claude ├── CHANGELOG.md Version history (semver, conventional commits) ├── LICENSE MIT license ├── .env.example Configuration template with all env vars ├── package.json Root package (canonical version = 1.0.1) ├── docker-compose.yml Production 4-service stack ├── docker-compose.dev.yml Dev stack (single bot container, temp vault) ├── .github/ GitHub Actions workflows ├── docs/ User documentation │ ├── INSTALLATION.md Setup walkthrough for local & remote │ ├── WORKFLOW.md How to write research requests & use callouts │ ├── CONFIGURATION.md All env vars reference │ ├── NOTIFICATIONS.md Discord & Twilio WhatsApp setup │ ├── OBSIDIAN_SETUP.md Obsidian Sync & template plugin config │ └── DEVELOPMENT.md Testing & adding new watchers ├── services/ Core application code │ ├── bot/ Main polling bot & all 5 watchers │ ├── obsidian-bridge/ Vault file watcher → Quartz builder │ └── obsidian-headless/ Obsidian Sync daemon (remote only) ├── vault-scaffold/ Default vault structure & prompt templates ├── templates/ Obsidian template files ├── tests/ Unit & integration test suites ├── scripts/ Automation (release.sh for semver bumping) └── vault-scaffold/ └── research/ ├── inbox/ User research requests go here ├── processed/ Completed requests (archive) ├── *.md Prompts (research-, amend-, expand-, revise-, lint-) ├── lint-trigger.md Audit trigger file ├── journal.md (seeded but empty initially) └── password-managers/ Example completed research topic ``` --- ### 2. DATA MODEL **Note Storage Format:** - Markdown files with YAML frontmatter - File-based, no database — stored in Obsidian vault directory - All data flows through the vault; no separate state store **Standard Frontmatter Fields:** ```yaml --- tags: [research-request] # Tag to identify what kind of file created: 2026-04-17 # ISO date when created status: pending|processing|completed|error|awaiting-input updated: 2026-04-17 # Last modified date sources: # Bibliography (list of URLs + titles) - url: https://... title: "Source Title" related: ["[[wikilink]]", "[[other]]"] # Cross-references (optional) publish: true|false # Quartz publication flag --- ``` **Research Topic Structure:** ``` research/ ├── index.md Topic overview, TL;DR, sub-page index ├── options-comparison.md Sub-pages for aspects worth their own section ├── tradeoffs.md (user decides granularity) ├── self-hosting-guide.md └── ...other sub-pages ``` **Research Request (in inbox/):** - Markdown file with `status: pending` and `research-request` tag - Content is the seed note (topic, scope, context) - Watcher moves it to `processed/` when starting, updates status to `processing` - Final status set to `completed` or `awaiting-input` (if Claude needs clarification) **Callout Syntax (inline tasks):** ```markdown > [!claude] > Edit instruction text here > [!expand] > Brief for new sub-page to create > [!revise] > Context for corpus-wide revisions ``` **Note-to-Note Links:** - Obsidian wikilink format: `[[research/topic-name/page|display text]]` - No special ID system — just file paths relative to vault root - Backlinks inferred from mention in markdown files **Karpathy Ingest Pass:** - Final step of research inbox watcher - Claude re-reads existing research tree and adds backlinks from new topic to related existing pages - Described in `research/research-prompt.md` (Step 5) - Ensures new knowledge propagates backward into the knowledge graph, not just forward --- ### 3. SERVICES/COMPONENTS **Service Architecture: 4 Docker containers** | Service | Container | Role | Runs on | Port | |---|---|---|---|---| | `rockybot` (bot) | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot:latest` | Main watcher loop, runs all Claude tasks | Always | Internal | | `rockybot-bridge` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge:latest` | Watches vault, builds Quartz static site | Always | Internal | | `rockybot-headless` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless:latest` | Obsidian Sync daemon (remote deployments only) | Remote only | Internal | | `notes-web` | `nginx:alpine` | Serves built Quartz site | Always | `8080` | **Bot Watchers (all serialized by global mutex in `index.js`):** Each watcher runs every 10 minutes (default `INBOX_POLL_SECONDS=600`). Only one Claude invocation at a time. | Watcher | File | Trigger | Claude Task | Budget | Model | |---|---|---|---|---|---| | **inbox** | `inbox-watcher.js` | `research/inbox/*.md` with `status: pending` | Full multi-page research + Karpathy backlink pass | `$2.00` | `sonnet` | | **amend** | `amend-watcher.js` | `> [!claude]` callout in any research page | Targeted inline edit per instruction | `$0.50` | `haiku` | | **expand** | `expand-watcher.js` | `> [!expand]` callout | Create new sub-page, replace callout with wikilink | `$1.00` | `sonnet` | | **revise** | `revise-watcher.js` | `> [!revise]` callout in topic `index.md` | Corpus-level rewrite: find all wikilinked sub-pages, revise each in-place | `$4.00` | `sonnet` | | **lint** | `lint-watcher.js` | `status: pending` in `research/lint-trigger.md` | Full wiki audit: orphaned pages, broken wikilinks, write report | `$5.00` | `sonnet` | **Polling Loop:** - Main loop in `services/bot/src/index.js` - Global mutex (`globalRunning` flag) — only one Claude invocation at a time - Serial execution: `scanInbox()` → `scanAmendments()` → `scanExpands()` → `scanRevisions()` → `scanLint()` - Runs on interval (default 10 min), continues forever **Obsidian Bridge:** - Watches vault with chokidar (5s polling, 3s write-finish debounce) - Listens for `.md` file changes - Debounced call to Quartz builder - On each build: reads `publish: true` flags from topic `index.md` files, syncs published topics to Quartz, builds static site **Quartz Integration:** - Quartz v4.4.0 baked into bridge image at build time (pinned, no internet at runtime) - Bridge invokes `npx quartz build --output /quartz-output` - Serves generated static site via nginx on port `8080` - Wikilinks converted to HTML by Quartz; `.md` files become `.html` --- ### 4. FRONTEND: STATIC NOTES WEBSITE **Framework:** - Quartz (open-source static site generator for Obsidian vaults) - No dynamic frontend; all content is pre-rendered HTML, CSS, JavaScript **Generated Site Structure:** - Root index: auto-generated by bridge from published topics - Topic pages: `/notes.{domain}/{topic-slug}/` (published topics only) - Sub-pages: `/notes.{domain}/{topic-slug}/{page-slug}/` - Wikilinks: converted to relative HTML links - Full-text search, backlink graph, sidebar navigation (Quartz defaults) **How Notes & Links Render:** - Obsidian wikilinks `[[research/topic/page|text]]` → Quartz converts to `text` - Publish gate: only topics with `publish: true` in their `index.md` are included in Quartz build - Markdown processed to HTML; frontmatter stripped except tags - Quartz plugins: graph view, sidebar, breadcrumbs, search (configurable) **Webserver:** - nginx:alpine container - Simple config in `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` - Listens on `0.0.0.0:8080` - Serves `/usr/share/nginx/html/` (mapped to `/quartz-output/` from bridge) - Caching headers on static assets (7-day cache for JS, CSS, images) --- ### 5. DATABASE **No database.** - File-based only: vault is the entire state store - Markdown files + directory structure - All state persisted via file writes (`fs-extra` library) - No locks, no transactions, no ACID — relies on OS file atomicity and Obsidian Sync (or direct mount) for consistency **LiveSync (self-hosted sync alternative) not supported:** - CLAUDE.md explains why: Live Sync's conflict resolution can corrupt status fields during concurrent watchers - Obsidian Sync (official paid service) is the recommended remote sync solution - Local setup: direct directory mount (e.g., NFS, SSHFS, or same-machine mount) --- ### 7. DOCKER/DEPLOYMENT **Production Stack (`docker-compose.yml`):** ```yaml obsidian-headless: # Obsidian Sync daemon (remote only) rockybot-bridge: # Vault watcher + Quartz builder notes-web: # nginx serving Quartz output rockybot: # Main polling bot ``` **Docker Images:** - Canonical source: https://ghcr.io/mrwong/ (GitHub Container Registry) - Built from GitHub Actions on tag push - Tags: `latest` (stable), `dev`/`edge` (dev branch), `vX.Y.Z` (releases), `vX.Y` (latest patch) **Volumes:** - `./data/vault:/vault` — shared Obsidian vault - `./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output` — generated static site - `./data/obsidian-config:/config` — headless Obsidian config (remote only) - `~/.claude:/home/ubuntu/.claude` — Claude subscription session (optional, Pro/Max billing) **Environment Variables:** - All configuration via `.env` (copied from `.env.example`) - Sourced into `docker compose` via `--env-file` - No CLI flags; name: Rockybot Architecture Reference
description: Full codebase map for rockybot — services, data model, watchers, Quartz/notes-web, key file paths. Read this at session start instead of re-exploring.
type: reference
---

## Rockybot — Architecture Reference

**Repo:** `/home/ubuntu/src/rockybot`  
**Version (as of last update):** 1.0.1

---

### Top-Level Structure

```
/services/bot/            Main polling bot + all 5 watchers
/services/obsidian-bridge/  Vault file watcher → Quartz static site builder
/services/obsidian-headless/  Obsidian Sync daemon (remote deployments only)
/vault-scaffold/          Default vault structure + prompt templates (baked into bot image)
/docs/                    User-facing documentation
/tests/                   Unit + integration tests
/scripts/release.sh       Semver bump + CHANGELOG + git tag automation
docker-compose.yml        Production 4-service stack
docker-compose.dev.yml    Dev stack (single container, temp vault, tight budgets)
.env.example              All env vars with defaults
```

---

### Data Model

**Storage:** Markdown files with YAML frontmatter — no database. Vault directory is the single source of truth.

**Standard frontmatter fields:**
```yaml
tags: [research-request]
created: 2026-04-17
status: pending|processing|completed|error|awaiting-input
updated: 2026-04-17
sources:
  - url: https://...
    title: "Source Title"
related: ["[[wikilink]]", "[[other]]"]
publish: true|false   # Quartz publication gate
```

**Research topic layout:**
```
research/<topic>/
├── index.md            Overview, TL;DR, sub-page list (publish: true here gates Quartz)
├── options-comparison.md
├── tradeoffs.md
└── ...other sub-pages
```

**Note-to-note links:** Obsidian wikilink format `[[research/topic/page|display text]]` — file-path relative to vault root. No separate ID system.

**Callout syntax (inline task triggers):**
```
> [!claude]    → amend-watcher: targeted inline edit
> [!expand]    → expand-watcher: create new sub-page
> [!revise]    → revise-watcher: corpus-wide rewrite of topic
```

---

### Services / Docker Stack

| Service | Image | Role | Port |
|---|---|---|---|
| `rockybot` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot` | Main polling bot, all Claude tasks | internal |
| `rockybot-bridge` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge` | Vault watcher → Quartz build | internal |
| `notes-web` | `nginx:alpine` | Serves built Quartz static site | 8080 |
| `rockybot-headless` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless` | Obsidian Sync daemon (remote only) | internal |

**Shared volumes:**
- `./data/vault:/vault` — vault (shared between bot, bridge, headless)
- `./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output` — built site (shared between bridge, notes-web)

---

### Bot Watchers

All watchers serialized by global mutex in `index.js`. Serial execution order: inbox → amend → expand → revise → lint.

| Watcher | File | Trigger | Budget | Model |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **inbox** | `inbox-watcher.js` | `research/inbox/*.md` with `status: pending` | $2.00 | sonnet |
| **amend** | `amend-watcher.js` | `> [!claude]` callout anywhere | $0.50 | haiku |
| **expand** | `expand-watcher.js` | `> [!expand]` callout | $1.00 | sonnet |
| **revise** | `revise-watcher.js` | `> [!revise]` in topic `index.md` | $4.00 | sonnet |
| **lint** | `lint-watcher.js` | `status: pending` in `research/lint-trigger.md` | $5.00 | sonnet |

Inbox watcher runs a **Karpathy ingest pass** as its final step: Claude re-reads existing research tree and adds backlinks from new topic to related existing pages.

---

### Claude Integration

**No REST API.** Claude invoked as subprocess via `claude --print <prompt>`.

- **Runner:** `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — spawns CLI, collects stdout/stderr, never rejects
- **Billing:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (default) or `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` (subscription first, API fallback)
- **Interactive auth:** If subscription fails and `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, posts OAuth URL to Discord as button; 5-min timeout before fallback
- **Budget:** `--max-budget-usd` flag (API-key mode only; subscription mode ignores budget flag)
- **Prompts:** Loaded from vault `research/*-prompt.md`; user-editable in Obsidian; hardcoded fallbacks if missing

---

### Notes-Web / Quartz Frontend

**Not a dynamic web app.** Pre-rendered static HTML by Quartz v4.4.0.

- **Bridge** (`services/obsidian-bridge/src/`) watches vault with chokidar (5s poll, 3s write-debounce), runs Quartz build on change
- **Publish gate:** Only topics with `publish: true` in their `index.md` are included in build
- **Link rendering:** Wikilinks `[[topic/page]]` → relative HTML links `<a href="/topic/page/">`
- **Features:** Full-text search, backlink graph, sidebar navigation (Quartz defaults)
- **nginx config:** `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`, serves `/quartz-output/` on port 8080, 7-day cache on static assets

---

### Key File Paths

**Bot core:**
- `services/bot/src/index.js` — main loop, global mutex, watcher orchestration
- `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — Claude subprocess + auth fallback
- `services/bot/src/notifier.js` — Discord/Twilio notifications
- `services/bot/src/discord-bot.js` — Discord WebSocket bot (interactive auth)
- `services/bot/src/login-runner.js` — `claude login` OAuth URL capture

**Watchers:**
- `services/bot/src/inbox-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/amend-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/expand-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/revise-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/lint-watcher.js`

**Bridge:**
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` — chokidar watcher setup
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` — Quartz build + publish gate
- `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`

**Vault scaffold (user-editable prompts):**
- `vault-scaffold/research/research-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/amend-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/expand-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/revise-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/lint-prompt.md`

**Docs (human-facing):**
- `docs/INSTALLATION.md`, `docs/WORKFLOW.md`, `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`, `docs/NOTIFICATIONS.md`, `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md`

---

### Release Process

1. `./scripts/release.sh` on `main` — shows commits since last tag, proposes semver bump
2. User confirms → script updates `services/bot/package.json` version + `CHANGELOG.md`, creates git tag
3. Push tag → GitHub Actions builds & pushes images to GHCR
4. Image tags: `latest`, `vX.Y.Z`, `vX.Y`
5. `dev` branch → `edge`/`dev` images (pre-release)

**Conventional commits:** `feat:` → minor, `fix:` → patch, `feat!:`/`fix!:` → major, `docs:`/`chore:`/`test:` → no bump

---

### Key Design Decisions

- **No database:** Vault files are source of truth; direct Obsidian access
- **Global mutex:** Prevents concurrent Claude runs + vault conflicts
- **File-based state:** Status in frontmatter survives restarts/crashes
- **Prompts in vault:** User-customizable without redeploying
- **Subscription + API fallback:** Layer budget strategies
- **Quartz static site:** Zero-cost hosting; opt-in publish flag per topic
- **Obsidian Sync (not LiveSync):** LiveSync conflict resolution breaks status frontmatter fields

---
name: Rockybot release — sync docs, changelog, and memory
description: When finalizing rockybot changes (release or edge push), always update reference memory, human docs, and CHANGELOG together.
type: feedback
---

When completing any rockybot feature or fix that changes core functionality — at the point of running `./scripts/release.sh` OR pushing to the `dev`/`edge` branch — always do all three of the following before or alongside the release:

1. **Update `reference_rockybot_architecture.md`** — reflect any changes to services, watchers, data model, key file paths, env vars, or design decisions. This is the memory read at session start; it must stay current.

2. **Update human-facing docs** (`docs/` folder) — update whichever docs are affected: `WORKFLOW.md` for new user-facing behaviors, `CONFIGURATION.md` for new env vars, `DEVELOPMENT.md` for new watcher patterns, etc.

3. **Update `CHANGELOG.md`** — add an entry under the new version (or `[Unreleased]`) describing what changed. `./scripts/release.sh` handles version bumping but expects the changelog entry to already be written.

**Why:** Without this, memory drifts from reality and future sessions give stale recommendations. Docs and changelog are the human equivalent of memory — they must stay in sync with the code.

**How to apply:** Treat these three updates as mandatory steps in the rockybot "definition of done," same as writing tests. If a change is too small to warrant a doc update (e.g., a one-line bug fix), the changelog entry is still required.

# Plan: Topic Export Feature for Rockybot

## Context

Users want to share a research topic (a Quartz-rendered tree of notes) with people who may not have access to the live notes-web server. The export must be portable and self-contained: internal links within the topic navigate between pages, while links to other topics are gracefully degraded. The feature should be accessible directly from the notes-web UI.

---

## Export Format: Self-Contained HTML ZIP

**Recommended format:** A ZIP file per topic containing the topic's rendered HTML pages + Quartz static assets (CSS, JS, fonts), with cross-topic links neutered.

**Why this format:**
- Quartz already renders the topic to high-quality HTML — no re-rendering needed
- ZIP works in every browser via native extract or dedicated apps
- Self-contained: recipient needs no server, no Obsidian, no special tools
- Preserves Quartz formatting, search (within-ZIP), and sidebar

**Cross-topic links:** `href` attributes pointing outside the topic are removed; the link text remains. A `title` attribute is added so hovering shows "External topic (not included in export)".

**Not chosen:** Markdown ZIP (requires Obsidian to render), PDF (links die), single-file HTML (Quartz JS won't work without a real URL structure).

---

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1 — Add dependencies to `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json`

Add two packages:
- `archiver` — ZIP creation
- `cheerio` — HTML parsing for link/path rewriting

### Step 2 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js`

Exports one function: `generateExports(quartzOutput, topics)`

**Internal logic:**

```
generateExports(quartzOutput, topics)
  → for each topic slug:
      generateTopicExport(quartzOutput, topic)

generateTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug)
  1. Ensure quartzOutput/exports/ dir exists
  2. Create archiver ZIP stream → quartzOutput/exports/<topicSlug>.zip
  3. Walk all files under quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/
       For each .html file:
         - Compute depth (0 for files directly under topic dir, 1 for subdirs, etc.)
         - Call rewriteHtml(content, topicSlug, depth)
         - Add rewritten content to ZIP at path stripped of topic prefix
           e.g. quartzOutput/password-managers/bitwarden/index.html → bitwarden/index.html
  4. Copy quartzOutput/static/ into ZIP at static/
  5. Finalize ZIP

rewriteHtml(htmlContent, topicSlug, depth)
  Uses cheerio to rewrite:

  a) Asset paths (src/href for CSS, JS, fonts, images):
     /static/foo.js → {prefix}static/foo.js   where prefix = "../".repeat(depth)

  b) Within-topic page links:
     href="/topicSlug/sub-page" → href="{prefix}sub-page/index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/index"   → href="{prefix}index.html"

  c) Cross-topic links:
     href starts with "/" but NOT with "/topicSlug/" → remove href,
     add title="External topic (not included in export)"
     add class="export-external-link" (for optional CSS styling)

  d) Anchor-only links (#section), relative hrefs, mailto: → leave unchanged
```

**ZIP output location:** `quartzOutput/exports/<topicSlug>.zip`

### Step 3 — Wire into `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js`

After the existing `chmod` / permission fix lines (end of `runBuild`), add:

```javascript
const { generateExports } = require('./export-builder');
// ...after chmod lines:
await generateExports(quartzOutput, topics);
logger.info('Export ZIPs generated');
```

Also add download links to the generated root `index.md`. Change:
```javascript
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => `- [[${t}/index|${t.replace(/-/g, ' ')}]]`).join('\n');
```
to:
```javascript
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => {
  const label = t.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase());
  return `- [[${t}/index|${label}]] · [⬇ Export ZIP](/exports/${t}.zip)`;
}).join('\n');
```

### Step 4 — nginx: no changes needed

nginx already serves everything under `/quartz-output/`. The `/exports/` subdirectory will be served automatically at `http://notes.{domain}/exports/<topic>.zip`.

### Step 5 — Memory update

After implementation, update `reference_rockybot_architecture.md` to document the export-builder module and ZIP output path.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json` | Add `archiver`, `cheerio` dependencies |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` | **New file** — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` | Call `generateExports` post-build; add download links to root index |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile` | No change needed (`npm install` picks up new deps) |
| `docs/WORKFLOW.md` | Document the export feature for users |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Add entry under [Unreleased] |

---

## Verification

1. Build the bridge image locally: `docker build -t rockybot-bridge-test services/obsidian-bridge/`
2. Run the stack with a vault that has at least one `publish: true` topic
3. Wait for a Quartz build to complete (watch logs for "Export ZIPs generated")
4. Confirm `data/quartz-output/exports/<topic>.zip` exists
5. Download the ZIP via browser at `http://localhost:8080/exports/<topic>.zip`
6. Extract and open `index.html` locally — verify:
   - Topic index page renders with Quartz styling
   - Sub-page links navigate correctly (relative paths work)
   - Cross-topic links render as plain non-clickable text
   - Static assets (CSS, JS) load from the local `static/` folder
7. Verify the download link appears on the notes-web root index page next to each topic

input.content:
# Plan: Topic Export Feature for Rockybot

## Context

Users want to share a research topic (a Quartz-rendered tree of notes) with people who may not have access to the live notes-web server. The export must be portable and self-contained: internal links within the topic navigate between pages, links to other topics are gracefully degraded, and the download is triggered directly from the notes-web UI. The existing bridge container handles generation; nginx proxies the export request to it.

---

## Export Format: Self-Contained HTML ZIP (on-demand)

**Format:** A ZIP file per topic containing the topic's rendered HTML pages + Quartz static assets (CSS, JS, fonts), with cross-topic links neutered. Generated on-demand when the user clicks "Export" — not pre-built.

**Why on-demand instead of pre-generated:**
- Topic trees are small; ZIP generation is fast (sub-second for typical topics)
- No stale ZIPs sitting around; always reflects the latest build
- No extra disk space overhead

**Cross-topic links:** `href` attributes pointing outside the topic are removed; link text remains with `title="External topic (not included in export)"`.

---

## Architecture: How the Request Flows

```
Browser clicks "Export ZIP" link on notes-web page
  → GET /export/<topicSlug>  (hits nginx on port 8080)
  → nginx proxy_pass to bridge:3001/export/<topicSlug>
  → bridge validates slug, generates ZIP in memory, streams as download
  → browser receives file download
```

No new containers. Bridge gains a small HTTP server on port 3001 (internal only, not exposed to host).

---

## Security Design

This is the most critical part of the feature. All protections are enforced before any file is touched:

**1. Slug whitelist (primary gate)**
The topicSlug from the URL is validated against `getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH)` — the same function already used by the build system. This returns only directory names that exist in `research/` with `publish: true`. The raw request string is never used to construct a file path. If the slug isn't in the whitelist, return 404 immediately.

```javascript
const published = getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH); // ['password-managers', 'auth-systems', ...]
if (!published.includes(requestedSlug)) { return 404; }
// Only now do we use the slug — it came from the filesystem, not the request
const topicDir = path.join(QUARTZ_OUTPUT, requestedSlug); // safe
```

**2. Slug format enforcement (defense in depth)**
Even before the whitelist check, reject any slug that contains characters outside `[a-z0-9-]`. This blocks `../`, URL-encoded traversal, null bytes, and path separators before they reach any logic.

**3. File walk uses symlink-safe options**
When walking `quartzOutput/<topic>/` to collect files for the ZIP, use `fs.readdir` with explicit `withFileTypes` and skip any entries that are symlinks. Quartz output is generated by our own code and shouldn't contain symlinks, but we skip them defensively.

**4. ZIP paths are constructed from filesystem walk, not user input**
Every path added to the ZIP is derived from the directory walk of `quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/`, with the topic prefix stripped. No part of the URL request touches ZIP entry names.

**5. No access to vault, secrets, or host paths**
The export-builder only reads from `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/<topicSlug>/` and `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/static/`. These are Quartz-generated HTML — not the raw vault, not secrets, not environment variables. The bridge runs as non-root (`node` user) with no Docker socket, no privileged mode, no host paths outside the declared volumes.

**6. Concurrency guard**
If a request for `<topicSlug>` is already in progress, return 429. Prevents resource exhaustion from repeated rapid requests.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1 — Add dependencies to `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json`

```json
"archiver": "^7.0.0",
"cheerio": "^1.0.0"
```

### Step 2 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js`

Exports one async function: `buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, responseStream)`

**Logic:**

```
buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, res)
  1. Set response headers: Content-Type: application/zip,
     Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<topicSlug>-export.zip"
  2. Create archiver ZIP stream piped to res
  3. Walk all files under quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/ (no symlinks)
       For each .html file:
         - Compute depth (number of path segments relative to topic root)
         - Call rewriteHtml(content, topicSlug, depth)
         - Add to ZIP at path stripped of topic-slug prefix
           e.g. quartzOutput/password-managers/bitwarden/index.html → bitwarden/index.html

# Plan: Topic Export Feature for Rockybot

## Context

Users want to share a research topic (a Quartz-rendered tree of notes) with people who may not have access to the live notes-web server. The export must be portable and self-contained: internal links within the topic navigate between pages, links to other topics are gracefully degraded, and the download is triggered directly from the notes-web UI. The existing bridge container handles generation; nginx proxies the export request to it.

---

## Export Format: Self-Contained HTML ZIP (on-demand)

**Format:** A ZIP file per topic containing the topic's rendered HTML pages + Quartz static assets (CSS, JS, fonts), with cross-topic links neutered. Generated on-demand when the user clicks "Export" — not pre-built.

**Why on-demand instead of pre-generated:**
- Topic trees are small; ZIP generation is fast (sub-second for typical topics)
- No stale ZIPs sitting around; always reflects the latest build
- No extra disk space overhead

**Cross-topic links:** `href` attributes pointing outside the topic are removed; link text remains with `title="External topic (not included in export)"`.

---

## Architecture: How the Request Flows

```
Browser clicks "Export ZIP" link on notes-web page
  → GET /export/<topicSlug>  (hits nginx on port 8080)
  → nginx proxy_pass to bridge:3001/export/<topicSlug>
  → bridge validates slug, generates ZIP in memory, streams as download
  → browser receives file download
```

No new containers. Bridge gains a small HTTP server on port 3001 (internal only, not exposed to host).

---

## Security Design

This is the most critical part of the feature. All protections are enforced before any file is touched:

**1. Slug whitelist (primary gate)**
The topicSlug from the URL is validated against `getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH)` — the same function already used by the build system. This returns only directory names that exist in `research/` with `publish: true`. The raw request string is never used to construct a file path. If the slug isn't in the whitelist, return 404 immediately.

```javascript
const published = getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH); // ['password-managers', 'auth-systems', ...]
if (!published.includes(requestedSlug)) { return 404; }
// Only now do we use the slug — it came from the filesystem, not the request
const topicDir = path.join(QUARTZ_OUTPUT, requestedSlug); // safe
```

**2. Slug format enforcement (defense in depth)**
Even before the whitelist check, reject any slug that contains characters outside `[a-z0-9-]`. This blocks `../`, URL-encoded traversal, null bytes, and path separators before they reach any logic.

**3. File walk uses symlink-safe options**
When walking `quartzOutput/<topic>/` to collect files for the ZIP, use `fs.readdir` with explicit `withFileTypes` and skip any entries that are symlinks. Quartz output is generated by our own code and shouldn't contain symlinks, but we skip them defensively.

**4. ZIP paths are constructed from filesystem walk, not user input**
Every path added to the ZIP is derived from the directory walk of `quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/`, with the topic prefix stripped. No part of the URL request touches ZIP entry names.

**5. No access to vault, secrets, or host paths**
The export-builder only reads from `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/<topicSlug>/` and `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/static/`. These are Quartz-generated HTML — not the raw vault, not secrets, not environment variables. The bridge runs as non-root (`node` user) with no Docker socket, no privileged mode, no host paths outside the declared volumes.

**6. Concurrency guard**
If a request for `<topicSlug>` is already in progress, return 429. Prevents resource exhaustion from repeated rapid requests.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1 — Add dependencies to `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json`

```json
"archiver": "^7.0.0",
"cheerio": "^1.0.0"
```

### Step 2 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js`

Exports one async function: `buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, responseStream)`

**Logic:**

```
buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, res)
  1. Set response headers: Content-Type: application/zip,
     Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<topicSlug>-export.zip"
  2. Create archiver ZIP stream piped to res
  3. Walk all files under quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/ (no symlinks)
       For each .html file:
         - Compute depth (number of path segments relative to topic root)
         - Call rewriteHtml(content, topicSlug, depth)
         - Add to ZIP at path stripped of topic-slug prefix
           e.g. quartzOutput/password-managers/bitwarden/index.html → bitwarden/index.html
  4. Copy quartzOutput/static/ into ZIP at static/ (skip symlinks)
  5. Finalize ZIP

rewriteHtml(htmlContent, topicSlug, depth)
  Uses cheerio. prefix = "../".repeat(depth)

  a) Static asset paths (src/href starting with /static/):
     /static/foo.js → {prefix}static/foo.js

  b) Within-topic page links:
     href="/topicSlug/sub-page" → href="{prefix}sub-page/index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/index"   → href="{prefix}index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/"        → href="{prefix}index.html"

  c) Cross-topic page links:
     href starts with "/" but not with "/<topicSlug>/"
     → remove href attribute
     → add title="External topic (not included in export)"
     → add class="export-external-link"

  d) Anchor-only (#section), mailto:, relative paths → unchanged
```

### Step 3 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js`

Small Node `http` server (no Express needed):

```
createExportServer(port, quartzOutput, vaultPath)
  Listens on 0.0.0.0:port (internal Docker port, not exposed to host)

  Routes:
    GET /export/<slug>
      1. Validate slug: reject if not /^[a-z0-9-]+$/
      2. Check slug in getPublishedTopics(vaultPath) → 404 if not found
      3. Check concurrency guard → 429 if already building this slug
      4. Call buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, slug, res)

    All other paths → 404
```

### Step 4 — Wire HTTP server into `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js`

After `configureQuartz()` and before the chokidar watcher:

```javascript
const { createExportServer } = require('./http-server');
createExportServer(3001, QUARTZ_OUTPUT, VAULT_PATH);
logger.info('Export HTTP server listening on :3001');
```

### Step 5 — Update `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`

Add a proxy block inside the existing `server {}`:

```nginx
location /export/ {
    proxy_pass http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/;
    proxy_read_timeout 30s;
    proxy_buffering off;
}
```

Notes-web (nginx) and the bridge are on the same Docker network and can reach each other by service name.

### Step 6 — Add export links to the generated root index in `quartz-builder.js`

Change the `topicLinks` generation:

```javascript
// Before:
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => `- [[${t}/index|${t.replace(/-/g, ' ')}]]`).join('\n');

// After:
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => {
  const label = t.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase());
  return `- [[${t}/index|${label}]] · [⬇ Export ZIP](/export/${t})`;
}).join('\n');
```

### Step 7 — `docker-compose.yml`: expose bridge's port 3001 internally

Add to the `rockybot-bridge` service (no host port mapping needed — nginx reaches it by service name):

```yaml
rockybot-bridge:
  expose:
    - "3001"
```

---

## Critical Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json` | Add `archiver`, `cheerio` |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` | **New** — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` | **New** — HTTP API with slug validation + security gate |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` | Start HTTP server on port 3001 |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` | Add export links to root index generation |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` | Add `/export/` proxy_pass block |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Add `expose: ["3001"]` to bridge service |
| `docs/WORKFLOW.md` | Document the export feature |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Add entry |

---

## Verification

1. Start the stack locally with `docker compose up`
2. Add a topic with `publish: true`, wait for Quartz build
3. Open `http://localhost:8080` — verify a "⬇ Export ZIP" link appears next to the topic
4. Click the link — browser should immediately start downloading `<topic>-export.zip`
5. Extract the ZIP and open `index.html` in a browser (file://)
6. Verify:
   - Topic index renders with Quartz styling (CSS/JS from local `static/`)
   - Sub-page links navigate correctly between pages in the ZIP
   - Cross-topic links render as non-clickable text with tooltip
7. Security spot-checks:
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/../etc/passwd` → 404
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/unpublished-topic` → 404

# Plan: Topic Export Feature for Rockybot

## Context

Users want to share a research topic (a Quartz-rendered tree of notes) with people who may not have access to the live notes-web server. The export must be portable and self-contained: internal links within the topic navigate between pages, while links to other topics are gracefully degraded. The feature should be accessible directly from the notes-web UI.

---

## Export Format: Self-Contained HTML ZIP

**Recommended format:** A ZIP file per topic containing the topic's rendered HTML pages + Quartz static assets (CSS, JS, fonts), with cross-topic links neutered.

**Why this format:**
- Quartz already renders the topic to high-quality HTML — no re-rendering needed
- ZIP works in every browser via native extract or dedicated apps
- Self-contained: recipient needs no server, no Obsidian, no special tools
- Preserves Quartz formatting, search (within-ZIP), and sidebar

**Cross-topic links:** `href` attributes pointing outside the topic are removed; the link text remains. A `title` attribute is added so hovering shows "External topic (not included in export)".

**Not chosen:** Markdown ZIP (requires Obsidian to render), PDF (links die), single-file HTML (Quartz JS won't work without a real URL structure).

---

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1 — Add dependencies to `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json`

Add two packages:
- `archiver` — ZIP creation
- `cheerio` — HTML parsing for link/path rewriting

### Step 2 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js`

Exports one function: `generateExports(quartzOutput, topics)`

**Internal logic:**

```
generateExports(quartzOutput, topics)
  → for each topic slug:
      generateTopicExport(quartzOutput, topic)

generateTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug)
  1. Ensure quartzOutput/exports/ dir exists
  2. Create archiver ZIP stream → quartzOutput/exports/<topicSlug>.zip
  3. Walk all files under quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/
       For each .html file:
         - Compute depth (0 for files directly under topic dir, 1 for subdirs, etc.)
         - Call rewriteHtml(content, topicSlug, depth)
         - Add rewritten content to ZIP at path stripped of topic prefix
           e.g. quartzOutput/password-managers/bitwarden/index.html → bitwarden/index.html
  4. Copy quartzOutput/static/ into ZIP at static/
  5. Finalize ZIP

rewriteHtml(htmlContent, topicSlug, depth)
  Uses cheerio to rewrite:

  a) Asset paths (src/href for CSS, JS, fonts, images):
     /static/foo.js → {prefix}static/foo.js   where prefix = "../".repeat(depth)

  b) Within-topic page links:
     href="/topicSlug/sub-page" → href="{prefix}sub-page/index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/index"   → href="{prefix}index.html"

  c) Cross-topic links:
     href starts with "/" but NOT with "/topicSlug/" → remove href,
     add title="External topic (not included in export)"
     add class="export-external-link" (for optional CSS styling)

  d) Anchor-only links (#section), relative hrefs, mailto: → leave unchanged
```

**ZIP output location:** `quartzOutput/exports/<topicSlug>.zip`

### Step 3 — Wire into `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js`

After the existing `chmod` / permission fix lines (end of `runBuild`), add:

```javascript
const { generateExports } = require('./export-builder');
// ...after chmod lines:
await generateExports(quartzOutput, topics);
logger.info('Export ZIPs generated');
```

Also add download links to the generated root `index.md`. Change:
```javascript
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => `- [[${t}/index|${t.replace(/-/g, ' ')}]]`).join('\n');
```
to:
```javascript
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => {
  const label = t.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase());
  return `- [[${t}/index|${label}]] · [⬇ Export ZIP](/exports/${t}.zip)`;
}).join('\n');
```

### Step 4 — nginx: no changes needed

nginx already serves everything under `/quartz-output/`. The `/exports/` subdirectory will be served automatically at `http://notes.{domain}/exports/<topic>.zip`.

### Step 5 — Memory update

After implementation, update `reference_rockybot_architecture.md` to document the export-builder module and ZIP output path.

---

## Critical Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json` | Add `archiver`, `cheerio` dependencies |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` | **New file** — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` | Call `generateExports` post-build; add download links to root index |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile` | No change needed (`npm install` picks up new deps) |
| `docs/WORKFLOW.md` | Document the export feature for users |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Add entry under [Unreleased] |

---

## Verification

1. Build the bridge image locally: `docker build -t rockybot-bridge-test services/obsidian-bridge/`
2. Run the stack with a vault that has at least one `publish: true` topic
3. Wait for a Quartz build to complete (watch logs for "Export ZIPs generated")
4. Confirm `data/quartz-output/exports/<topic>.zip` exists
5. Download the ZIP via browser at `http://localhost:8080/exports/<topic>.zip`
6. Extract and open `index.html` locally — verify:
   - Topic index page renders with Quartz styling
   - Sub-page links navigate correctly (relative paths work)
   - Cross-topic links render as plain non-clickable text
   - Static assets (CSS, JS) load from the local `static/` folder
7. Verify the download link appears on the notes-web root index page next to each topic

Users want to share a research topic (a Quartz-rendered tree of notes) with people who may not have access to the live notes-web server. The export must be portable and self-contained: internal links within the topic navigate between pages, links to other topics are gracefully degraded, and the download is triggered directly from the notes-web UI. The existing bridge container handles generation; nginx proxies the export request to it.

---

## Export Format: Self-Contained HTML ZIP (on-demand)

**Format:** A ZIP file per topic containing the topic's rendered HTML pages + Quartz static assets (CSS, JS, fonts), with cross-topic links neutered. Generated on-demand when the user clicks "Export" — not pre-built.

**Why on-demand instead of pre-generated:**
- Topic trees are small; ZIP generation is fast (sub-second for typical topics)
- No stale ZIPs sitting around; always reflects the latest build
- No extra disk space overhead

**Cross-topic links:** `href` attributes pointing outside the topic are removed; link text remains with `title="External topic (not included in export)"`.

---

## Architecture: How the Request Flows

```
Browser clicks "Export ZIP" link on notes-web page
  → GET /export/<topicSlug>  (hits nginx on port 8080)
  → nginx proxy_pass to bridge:3001/export/<topicSlug>
  → bridge validates slug, generates ZIP in memory, streams as download
  → browser receives file download
```

No new containers. Bridge gains a small HTTP server on port 3001 (internal only, not exposed to host).

---

## Security Design

This is the most critical part of the feature. All protections are enforced before any file is touched:

**1. Slug whitelist (primary gate)**
The topicSlug from the URL is validated against `getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH)` — the same function already used by the build system. This returns only directory names that exist in `research/` with `publish: true`. The raw request string is never used to construct a file path. If the slug isn't in the whitelist, return 404 immediately.

```javascript
const published = getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH); // ['password-managers', 'auth-systems', ...]
if (!published.includes(requestedSlug)) { return 404; }
// Only now do we use the slug — it came from the filesystem, not the request
const topicDir = path.join(QUARTZ_OUTPUT, requestedSlug); // safe
```

**2. Slug format enforcement (defense in depth)**
Even before the whitelist check, reject any slug that contains characters outside `[a-z0-9-]`. This blocks `../`, URL-encoded traversal, null bytes, and path separators before they reach any logic.

**3. File walk uses symlink-safe options**
When walking `quartzOutput/<topic>/` to collect files for the ZIP, use `fs.readdir` with explicit `withFileTypes` and skip any entries that are symlinks. Quartz output is generated by our own code and shouldn't contain symlinks, but we skip them defensively.

**4. ZIP paths are constructed from filesystem walk, not user input**
Every path added to the ZIP is derived from the directory walk of `quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/`, with the topic prefix stripped. No part of the URL request touches ZIP entry names.

**5. No access to vault, secrets, or host paths**
The export-builder only reads from `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/<topicSlug>/` and `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/static/`. These are Quartz-generated HTML — not the raw vault, not secrets, not environment variables. The bridge runs as non-root (`node` user) with no Docker socket, no privileged mode, no host paths outside the declared volumes.

**6. Concurrency guard**
If a request for `<topicSlug>` is already in progress, return 429. Prevents resource exhaustion from repeated rapid requests.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1 — Add dependencies to `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json`

```json
"archiver": "^7.0.0",
"cheerio": "^1.0.0"
```

### Step 2 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js`

Exports one async function: `buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, responseStream)`

**Logic:**

```
buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, res)
  1. Set response headers: Content-Type: application/zip,
     Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<topicSlug>-export.zip"
  2. Create archiver ZIP stream piped to res
  3. Walk all files under quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/ (no symlinks)
       For each .html file:
         - Compute depth (number of path segments relative to topic root)
         - Call rewriteHtml(content, topicSlug, depth)
         - Add to ZIP at path stripped of topic-slug prefix
           e.g. quartzOutput/password-managers/bitwarden/index.html → bitwarden/index.html
  4. Copy quartzOutput/static/ into ZIP at static/ (skip symlinks)
  5. Finalize ZIP

rewriteHtml(htmlContent, topicSlug, depth)
  Uses cheerio. prefix = "../".repeat(depth)

  a) Static asset paths (src/href starting with /static/):
     /static/foo.js → {prefix}static/foo.js

  b) Within-topic page links:
     href="/topicSlug/sub-page" → href="{prefix}sub-page/index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/index"   → href="{prefix}index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/"        → href="{prefix}index.html"

  c) Cross-topic page links:
     href starts with "/" but not with "/<topicSlug>/"
     → remove href attribute
     → add title="External topic (not included in export)"
     → add class="export-external-link"

  d) Anchor-only (#section), mailto:, relative paths → unchanged
```

### Step 3 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js`

Small Node `http` server (no Express needed):

```
createExportServer(port, quartzOutput, vaultPath)
  Listens on 0.0.0.0:port (internal Docker port, not exposed to host)

  Routes:
    GET /export/<slug>
      1. Validate slug: reject if not /^[a-z0-9-]+$/
      2. Check slug in getPublishedTopics(vaultPath) → 404 if not found
      3. Check concurrency guard → 429 if already building this slug
      4. Call buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, slug, res)

    All other paths → 404
```

### Step 4 — Wire HTTP server into `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js`

After `configureQuartz()` and before the chokidar watcher:

```javascript
const { createExportServer } = require('./http-server');
createExportServer(3001, QUARTZ_OUTPUT, VAULT_PATH);
logger.info('Export HTTP server listening on :3001');
```

### Step 5 — Update `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`

Add a proxy block inside the existing `server {}`:

```nginx
location /export/ {
    proxy_pass http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/;
    proxy_read_timeout 30s;
    proxy_buffering off;
}
```

Notes-web (nginx) and the bridge are on the same Docker network and can reach each other by service name.

### Step 6 — Add export links to the generated root index in `quartz-builder.js`

Change the `topicLinks` generation:

```javascript
// Before:
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => `- [[${t}/index|${t.replace(/-/g, ' ')}]]`).join('\n');

// After:
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => {
  const label = t.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase());
  return `- [[${t}/index|${label}]] · [⬇ Export ZIP](/export/${t})`;
}).join('\n');
```

### Step 7 — `docker-compose.yml`: expose bridge's port 3001 internally

Add to the `rockybot-bridge` service (no host port mapping needed — nginx reaches it by service name):

```yaml
rockybot-bridge:
  expose:
    - "3001"
```

---

## Critical Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json` | Add `archiver`, `cheerio` |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` | **New** — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` | **New** — HTTP API with slug validation + security gate |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` | Start HTTP server on port 3001 |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` | Add export links to root index generation |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` | Add `/export/` proxy_pass block |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Add `expose: ["3001"]` to bridge service |
| `docs/WORKFLOW.md` | Document the export feature |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Add entry |

---

## Verification

1. Start the stack locally with `docker compose up`
2. Add a topic with `publish: true`, wait for Quartz build
3. Open `http://localhost:8080` — verify a "⬇ Export ZIP" link appears next to the topic
4. Click the link — browser should immediately start downloading `<topic>-export.zip`
5. Extract the ZIP and open `index.html` in a browser (file://)
6. Verify:
   - Topic index renders with Quartz styling (CSS/JS from local `static/`)
   - Sub-page links navigate correctly between pages in the ZIP
   - Cross-topic links render as non-clickable text with tooltip
7. Security spot-checks:
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/../etc/passwd` → 404
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/unpublished-topic` → 404
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/` → 404

content.text:
Good question. The whitelist is **not static** — `getPublishedTopics()` (already in `quartz-builder.js`) reads the vault from disk on every call. In the HTTP server, it would be called fresh on each export request.

Here's the exact flow when you change `publish` status:

**`false` → `true`:**
- You save the change in Obsidian
- Chokidar picks it up (5s poll + 3s debounce), triggers a Quartz rebuild
- While rebuild is running, an export request for that topic returns 404 (not yet in whitelist — vault file reads `publish: true` but the function still won't find a Quartz-built HTML output to zip)
- After rebuild completes, topic is in both the Quartz site and the export whitelist

**`true` → `false`:**
- You save the change
- The next export request immediately returns 404 — `getPublishedTopics` reads the vault directly and the topic is gone from the whitelist before the Quartz rebuild even runs. This is the right behavior: revocation is instant.

The only wrinkle: there's a brief window (~5-8s) after you unpublish where the Quartz site still shows the topic (rebuild hasn't run yet) but exports are already blocked. That's the correct failure mode — export closes before the site updates.

I'll update the plan to explicitly state that `getPublishedTopics` must be called per-request (not cached), and note this behavior. Want me to make that change and re-submit for approval?

services:
  # Obsidian Sync daemon — keeps the vault directory in sync with Obsidian Sync cloud
  # Requires one-time interactive setup: see docs/OBSIDIAN_SETUP.md
  obsidian-headless:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless:latest
    container_name: rockybot-headless
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./data/vault:/vault
      - ./data/obsidian-config:/config

  # Obsidian Bridge — watches vault for changes and builds a Quartz static site
  obsidian-bridge:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge:latest
    container_name: rockybot-bridge
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - QUARTZ_OUTPUT=/quartz-output
      - DOMAIN_NAME=${DOMAIN_NAME:-localhost}
    volumes:
      - ./data/vault:/vault
      - ./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-headless

  # Notes Web — nginx serving the Quartz-built static site
  notes-web:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: rockybot-notes
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - ./data/quartz-output:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
      - ./services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-bridge

  # rockybot — watches vault inbox and callouts, calls Claude to process them
  bot:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot:latest
    container_name: rockybot
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - HOME=/home/ubuntu
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=${CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE:-false}
      - DRY_RUN=${DRY_RUN:-false}

      # Polling intervals (seconds)
      - INBOX_POLL_SECONDS=${INBOX_POLL_SECONDS:-600}

      # Spending caps per watcher invocation
      - RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD=${RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD:-2.00}
      - AMEND_BUDGET_USD=${AMEND_BUDGET_USD:-0.50}
      - EXPAND_BUDGET_USD=${EXPAND_BUDGET_USD:-1.00}
      - REVISE_BUDGET_USD=${REVISE_BUDGET_USD:-4.00}
      - LINT_BUDGET_USD=${LINT_BUDGET_USD:-5.00}

      # Claude model selection per watcher
      - AMEND_MODEL=${AMEND_MODEL:-haiku}
      - EXPAND_MODEL=${EXPAND_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - REVISE_MODEL=${REVISE_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - LINT_MODEL=${LINT_MODEL:-sonnet}

      # Notifications (optional — leave blank to disable)
      - DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL=${DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL:-}
      - TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=${TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID:-}
      - TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=${TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN:-}
      - TWILIO_FROM=${TWILIO_FROM:-}
      - TWILIO_TO=${TWILIO_TO:-}
    volumes:
      # Optional: mount Claude subscription session for Pro/Max billing
      # Comment out if using API key billing only (CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=false)
      - ~/.claude:/home/ubuntu/.claude
      - ./data/vault:/vault
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-headless
